授業目的: In this course is an all-English program, will focus on the most recent trends in three key areas - exploring human value, formulating business strategy, and developing technology platform for innovation. The course utilizes lectures, case studies, group exercises, guest speeches, company visits, and a group project to facilitate learning.
到達目標: Through taking this course, students are expected to achieve the following two goals:To develop a basic understanding of innovation management and relevant functional fields in business administration; and To acquire knowledge and hand-on experience on how to explore human value, how to formulate business strategy, and how to develop technology platform for innovation management and entrepreneurship.
授業方法: Participants must attend prior orientation and lecturers,On-site fieldwork will be conducted.Details will be announced at a late date.

トピック.授業計画1: Details will be announced at a later date.《2025minutes》
トピック.授業外学習2: Participate in NTU(National Taiwan University)'s Innovation & Entrepreneurship Spring Program.

学位授与の方針との関連Relationship to Diploma Polic: Each course is positioned within the curriculum in accordance with the diploma policy andcurriculum policy established by each undergraduate/graduate school. By proceeding with the study of this course with an awareness of the DP and CP, the following abilities expected by each undergraduate/graduate school will be developed.Asian Studies:1. Understanding and analytical skills regarding international issues: the ability to understand and analyze various regions of the world from different perspectives in the humanities and social sciences.2. Problem-solving skills: the ability to proactively identify problems and solve them based on appropriate methods.3. Intercultural understanding and multicultural coexistence: the ability to be sensitive to different cultures and to coexist with diverse cultures.
成績評価Grading0: 評価基準等　Grading Criteria etc.
成績評価Grading1: Attendance20%,Acrive Partcipation20%,Group Project Presentation30%,Group Project Report30%

name: 【200】Introduction to Business in Asia: Innovation, Leadership, and Strategy／Introduction to Business in Asia: Innovation, Leadership, and Strategy [EN]
